explanation: scroll-behavior: smooth; on html/body creates buttery-smooth jumps to anchors.

question_text: Which scroll-snap property activates snapping behavior on the container?
explanation: scroll-snap-type: y mandatory; makes the container snap to child positions on scroll.

question_text: What does touch-action: manipulation; do?
explanation: manipulation allows pan + pinch but disables double-tap zoom (faster 300ms click delay removal).

question_text: Why do some developers set overscroll-behavior: none; on body?
option_b: To prevent scroll chaining and overscroll glow effects
explanation: none stops the browser's default overscroll animation (rubber-band effect on mobile).

question_text: Which modern property sets a preferred aspect ratio for images or containers?
explanation: aspect-ratio: 16 / 9; prevents layout shift when images load.

question_text: What is a common use case for will-change: transform;?
option_a: To hint that the element will animate transform soon (promotes to GPU layer early)
explanation: will-change prepares compositor layer early — useful before heavy animations start.

question_text: Why might isolation: isolate; be used even without z-index?
option_a: To create a new stacking context for blend modes or opacity to work independently
explanation: isolation creates a stacking context — useful when you want opacity/blend-mode to not affect siblings.

question_text: When should you use padding instead of margin?
option_a: To add space outside the border

explanation: padding creates internal space inside the element.

question_text: Why is * { margin:0; padding:0; } commonly used at the start of a stylesheet?
option_b: To remove inconsistent browser default margins and padding
explanation: The universal selector reset eliminates unpredictable default spacing across browsers.

question_text: Which display value removes an element from the layout and frees its space?
explanation: display:none removes the element completely from the flow (no space reserved).

question_text: What happens when you apply visibility:hidden to an element?
option_a: It disappears and collapses the space
option_b: It disappears but keeps its space in the layout
explanation: visibility:hidden hides the element visually but reserves its layout space.
